entries.title: Outer Rim Sieges Stretch Jedi Thin

content.fact: The Outer Rim Sieges scatter Jedi and clone forces across distant campaigns, leaving Coruscant politically vulnerable.
content.injection: During the Outer Rim Sieges, many Jedi are away from the capital and worn down by extended campaigns. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Outer Rim Sieges.
Respect story-position bounds from Outer Rim Sieges to Order 66.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

content.fact: The Separatist strike on Coruscant is a late-war shock that brings the war directly to the Republic capital.
content.injection: During the Battle of Coruscant, portray the capital as under direct strategic threat, not merely watching a distant war.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Battle of Coruscant.
Respect story-position bounds from Battle of Coruscant to Dooku Dies Aboard Invisible Hand.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: Palpatine’s abduction aboard the Invisible Hand positions Anakin for a moral and political test engineered by Sidious.
content.injection: In the Coruscant rescue, frame Anakin’s choices as being manipulated by Palpatine, not as a random battlefield dilemma.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Battle of Coruscant.

entries.title: Dooku Death Removes Public Separatist Face

content.fact: Dooku’s death removes the elegant public face of the Separatist movement and clears Sidious’s apprentice line for Anakin.
content.injection: After Dooku dies, do not keep him available as active Separatist leadership; Grievous and the Council carry the visible war.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Dooku Death Before Empire.
Respect story-position bounds from Dooku Dies Aboard Invisible Hand to Galactic Empire Declared.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Anakin Council Seat Without Master Rank Wounds Him

content.fact: Anakin’s Council seat without Master rank deepens his resentment and sense that the Jedi do not trust him.
content.injection: In late ROTS context, use the Council decision as a personal wound feeding Anakin’s instability. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: ROTS Council Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Jedi Ask Anakin to Monitor Palpatine to Order 66.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Jedi Monitoring Palpatine Crosses Anakin Loyalty

content.fact: The Jedi request that Anakin monitor Palpatine, forcing him between institutional duty and personal loyalty.
content.injection: When this gate is active, show Anakin caught between Jedi suspicion and Palpatine’s grooming. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: ROTS Council Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Jedi Ask Anakin to Monitor Palpatine to Order 66.

content.fact: Anakin and Padme are secretly married after Geonosis; public exposure would threaten Anakin’s Jedi standing.
content.injection: Before Order 66, keep Anakin and Padme’s marriage secret from most characters and institutionally dangerous. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Secret Marriage Before Order 66.

entries.title: Anakin Fear For Padme Drives Fall

content.fact: Anakin’s fear of losing Padme makes Palpatine’s promise of forbidden power emotionally decisive.
content.injection: During Anakin’s fall, emphasize fear, possessive attachment, and desperation over simple ambition.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Anakin Fall Window.
Respect story-position bounds from Jedi Ask Anakin to Monitor Palpatine to Mustafar Duel and Vader's Ruin.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Palpatine Reveal To Anakin Precedes Order 66

content.fact: Palpatine reveals himself as Sith to Anakin before Order 66, but this is not public knowledge.
content.injection: After the reveal and before Order 66, only a narrow circle knows Palpatine is Sith; do not make the galaxy aware.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Before Order 66 Reveal Window.
Respect story-position bounds from Jedi Ask Anakin to Monitor Palpatine to Order 66.

content.fact: The Jedi confrontation with Palpatine is quickly reframed by Sidious as a Jedi coup, but ordinary citizens do not witness the full Sith context.
content.injection: After the confrontation, public scenes should reflect Palpatine’s coup narrative, not perfect knowledge of what happened in his office.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Palpatine Arrest Attempt.

entries.title: Order 66 Turns Clone Loyalty Against Jedi

content.fact: Order 66 converts the clone army from Jedi-led Republic force into the immediate instrument of Jedi extermination.
content.injection: During Order 66, clone units should abruptly treat Jedi as lawful targets under Supreme Chancellor command. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Order 66.
Respect story-position bounds from Order 66 to Galactic Empire Declared.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

sourceInfo.notes: Generated development Lorecard for Saga; requires human canon review.

content.fact: The Jedi Temple falls rapidly during Operation Knightfall, turning the Order’s central sanctuary into a massacre site and Imperial prize.
content.injection: After Order 66 begins, do not treat the Jedi Temple as a safe headquarters or intact command center. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Order 66.

content.injection: After Mustafar, write Vader as armored, physically altered, and trapped in pain; do not write him as pre-suit Anakin.

entries.title: Obi-Wan And Yoda Go Into Exile

content.fact: After the fall of the Jedi, Obi-Wan and Yoda survive in exile rather than leading open Jedi resistance.
content.injection: After ROTS, keep Obi-Wan and Yoda hidden unless the scene deliberately centers their exile or secret guardianship.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Skywalker Children Hidden.

entries.title: Clone Wars End Is Not Clean Peace

content.fact: The Clone Wars end through Sith consolidation, Separatist decapitation, and Jedi extermination, not through a negotiated peace.
content.injection: After the Empire forms, treat the war’s end as authoritarian victory and cover-up rather than reconciliation. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Early Empire.
Respect story-position bounds from Galactic Empire Declared to Dark Times and Imperial Consolidation.
